def: s.m. Musician; band of musicians; — s.f. A tax exacted (under Moḥammadan rule) from musicians, professional singers, and dancing girls
more: A musician; a band of musicians. Bāje de, bajantarī, kalī kukrī mat chheṛ, Tujhe birānī kyā paṛī, tū apnī to nabeṛ . Kabīr. Let, let the lyrist play, why jeer and mock the lyre rude? Your own work rightly do, why on another's lines intrude? bājantrī, yā bajantrī mahāl , n. m. 1. The quarter inhabited by musicians. 2. A tax under the Mahomedan Government imposed upon all professional singers, dancers, and musicians.
subs: s.m. The quarter of a town inhabited by musicians.

def: adj. Having a projecting navel; afflicted with a disease of the navel; having a projecting belly; pot-bellied.
more: n. m. S वज्ज 1. A hard heavy stone; diamond; adamant. 1. Lohe kī koṭhrī, bajjar ke kiwāṛ . Kahānī. 2. Kām koṛhī, muṅh bajjar . Prov. To work a leper maimed, to eat a mouth of stone. 2. The thunderbolt of Indra; lightning. 1. Piu daras kī bhīk na pāī, Bajar paṛe yā des, re! Song. Ṭhumrī . Mukhaīyar. My love long sought in vain, not found, May lightning blast the country round! 2. Āg lage maṅḍhe, bajjar paṛeṅ barātī . Fire burn the nuptial bower, may lightning strike the wedding party!

adj. 1. Hard; adamantine; impenetrable. 2. Heavy; weighty; ponderous; unwieldy. 1. Saīyāṅ chalal pardes, sabhe sukh le gaë, Chhatyan bajar kiwāṛ, jaṅjirwā lagāī gaë . E. Song. My husband gone abroad, all joy with him he took, Chained to my breast a door of stone and me forsook. 2. " Kholo kholo, pīyā, bajar kivaṛyā." "Kahāṅ gaṅwailu sārī ratyā re kī ." Mag. Song. "Please open, love, I pray, the heavy door." "The night where have you passed, in what amour?" 3. Slow; lazy; indolent; immovable. Hai itnā chalne meṅ bajjar yeh bad-zāt, Nahīṅ hāthī, saūbat kī hai yeh rāt . A satire on the king's elephant. Saudā. This rascal elephant doth drag as slow As doth the weary night of heavy woe. 4. Difficult ( kaṭhan ); not easy; laborious; unmanageable.
prep. With the exception, without, besides, except.

def: v.n. To sound (as a musical instrument, &c.); to strike (as a clock); to ring, resound ( = bajnā); to be published; to be known, be notorious or famous (as); — v.t. To sound, play upon, &c. = bajānā, q.v.
more: (from वाद्य see باجا) v. n. To sound (as a musical instrument); to be published, to resound.

def: v.n. To sound; to strike (as a clock, &c.); to be sounded; to be struck (as a gong, &c.), be played upon (as a musical instrument), be rung (as a bell), be fired (as a gun); to chatter (as the teeth); to ring, resound, be famous or celebrated; to be busy or active (as a sword, stick, &c.); to be generally known or called (by, -se, a name, &c.), to go by the name (of).
more: v. n. S. बजना to sound. 1. To give or emit sound; to sound; resound. 1. Thotā chanā, bāje ghanā . Prov. The hollow grain the loudest sounds. 2. Ab ḍing ḍaṅg bolā girjā meṅ, aur ṭhan ṭhan bājā mandar meṅ, Mullā jāgā masjid meṅ, aur bājā gājā mandar meṅ . Faqīr Chand. The Church bells go ding dong, dong ding, the temples ṭhan ṭhan ring, The Mullā in the masjid cries, the temples loudest din. 2. To be played upon or struck. 1. Tāṅt bājī, rāg pāyā . Prov. From the string a sound, the key note found. 2. Jantī, na ḍhol bajtā . Prov. No birth, no music. 3. To be fired (as a gun). Top baj gaī . 4. To be rung (a bell); to strike (a clock). Chār baje haiṅ . 5. To chatter (the teeth); shiver with cold. Dāṅt bajnā . 6. To be called, named, known. Woh kis nām se bājtā hai? 7. To be well known, famous, celebrated. bajnā-bajnī , Bhoj. n. f. A set to; a battle royal.
adj. Emitting sound; sounding; rattling. Ham to bajnā jhunjhunā leṅge . Children.
(intrans. of بجانا q. v.) 1. v. n. To be sounded, to sound, to be famous. 2. s. m. A rupee (used among brokers).
wety: From Sanskrit वाद्यते (vādyate).

def: s.m. A species of panic or millit, panicum spicatum (it is, par excellence, the 'food' of the poorer classes)
more: n. m. 1. A species of panic or millet; Panicum spicatum , Wat.; a small cheap grain which furnishes the meal cakes of the poorer classes. 1. Bājrā kahe, "maiṅ hūṅ albelā, Do mūsal sūṅ laṛūṅ akelā ." Song. Ṭesū . The Bājra says, "I am a valiant knight, With two grain-rammers great alone I fight." 2. Maiṅ bājrā kāṭaṅ jāūṅgī , W. Rus. Wom. Song. Āchhī āchhī bāl toṛ-kar bhūnūṅgī aur khāūṅgī . I'll go and cut some bājrā sweet, Nice nice grain stalks I'll roast and eat. 2. (Met.) Drizzle; small rain. Āj to bājrā baras rahā hai. bājrā sā bikharnā, lit . To be scattered like bājrā . 1. To be restless, uneasy, disturbed. 2. To be tired, fatigued, faint. bājrā-murg , n. m. A black fowl with yellow spots of the size of a grain of bājrā .
s. m. A kind of grain so called, Indian corn. (Holcus spicatus).
subs: 'To be scattered like bājrā'; to be restless, uneasy, disturbed; to be weary or tired
s.m. A speckled fowl (particularly a black fowl with yellow spots of the size of a grain of bājrā).

s. f. Name of a plant the seed of which is used to cure the itch. (Conyza or Serratula anthelmintica).

s.m. Root of a grass (mūnj, &c.) of which rope is made.
more: s. m. The root of a grass of which rope is made.

adv. Attended with good, in welfare, well, in safety; for good, to a good end or purpose; in peace and safety, alone and unmeddled with
more: adv. 1. Safe and sound; all right; well. 2. No no; not a bit of it; excuse me. Dil deṅge, jān deṅge, magar māl, so bakhair! Sheftah. My heart I'll give, my life I'll give, but let me keep my gold, I pray.
subs: adv. Safe and sound, well, in safety.

def: safely
wety: Equivalent to بَخَیر (baxair) + ـیَت (-iyat).

def: s.m. = bākhar, q.v. (Dialectic forms are bakkhal, bakhrī). s.m. House; collection of houses in one enclosure; a cluster of cattle-sheds; enclosure, area, court-yard; — a bag or wallet (for tools, — as a goldsmith's); — a kind of drug used as a ferment.
more: n. f. An enclosure, area, or court-yard comprising several houses, cattle-sheds, etc.
s. m. 1. An area or court yard. 2. Several houses contained within one enclosure. 3. Name of a drug used as a ferment.

ety: S. व्याख्यानं, or विख्यान, Prk. वक्खाणं
def: s.m. Explanation, exposition; definition, description, declaration, account, relation; gloss, comment, interpretation; naming, calling; praise, commendation
more: n. m. S. व्याख्यान, Pr. vakkāṅam , from ख्या to speak. 1. See برنن 2. Preaching. Bed kā bakhān . 3. Invocation; prayer; praise ( astutī ). 4. Flattery; adulation. Mūrakh ko bakhān suhāve . Prov. Flattery pleases fools.
s. m. Explanation, praise, description, definition. بکهان کرنا bakhān karnā, see بکهاننا bakhānnā.
subs: (-kā), To expound, explain, define, describe, declare, relate; to praise, commend ( = bakhānnā).

def: s.f. = bakhān, q.v. s.m. Explanation, exposition; definition, description, declaration, account, relation; gloss, comment, interpretation; naming, calling; praise, commendation
subs: (-kā), To expound, explain, define, describe, declare, relate; to praise, commend ( = bakhānnā).

def: v.t. To expound, &c. = bakhān karnā, q.v.s.v. bakhān
more: v. a. 1. To mention. See barnan karnā . Phr. To enter into detail; descend to particulars. Nirkhat rūp Sīyā, Raghubar ko, chhabi nahīṅ jāt bakhānī. Bhajan . Hymn. 2. To preach; pray; sing praises. 3. To give (the genealogy). Kulī bakhānnā . 4. To abuse; call names; vilify. Agar mere muṅh lagī, tau terī chhoṭī baṛī ko bakhān ḍālūṅgī . Mah. Wom. If I once begin, I'll brand all your kith and kin (with foul names).
v. a. 1. To praise, to commend. 2. To explain, to define, to relate.
subs: (-ko), To name with opprobrium, to vilify, abuse (used by women).

ety: S. वल्कलं? rt. वल्; Prk. वक्कलं
def: s.m. House; collection of houses in one enclosure; a cluster of cattle-sheds; enclosure, area, court-yard; — a bag or wallet (for tools, — as a goldsmith's); — a kind of drug used as a ferment.
more: s. m. 1. An area or court yard. 2. Several houses contained within one enclosure. 3. Name of a drug used as a ferment.

s.m. A kind of hoe or plough (bākhar, q.v.).

more: n. f. 1. Scattering. 2. The thing scattered.

ety: S. वि+कार, or कृत, rt. कृ+कः
def: s.m. Entanglement, complication, imbroglio, difficulty, hitch: any troublesome or vexatious business, affair or occurrence; trouble, care, trial, worry, agitation; obstacle, impediment, encumbrance, clog; (hence) goods, chattels, furniture; lumber, rubbish; disagreement, difference, dispute, contention, broil, wrangling, altercation, quarrel, brawl, disturbance, pother, tumult, bustle, uproar; — clever contrivance, dodge, resource, artifice
more: s. m. Wrangling, a tumult, broil, contention, dispute, quarrel.
subs: (-kā), To settle a dispute; to finish a troublesome business
bakheṛā karnā, bakheṛā maćānā (-meṅ), To complicate, make difficult or troublesome, throw obstacles (in the way of), raise or create a difficulty; to bring about a quarrel, &c., cause contention.

def: n. m. 1. Knot; entanglement; complication; mess; imbroglio. Phr. Wheels within wheels; most admired disorder; confusion worse confounded; a pretty kettle of fish; in a ferment. 2. Difficulty; trouble; mess; hitch. 3. Care; trouble; trial; sorrow. Sab jīte jī kā bakheṛā hai . Prov. While there is life there is strife. 4. Impediment; obstacle; stumbling-block; check; encumbrance; drag. 5. Difference; disagreement; schism; breach; rupture. 6. Doubt; demur; question; dispute. 7. Quarrel ( laṛāī ); contention ( jhagṛā ); wrangle; broil; turmoil; rumpus. Inshā Allāh Khāṅ ko, sāhib, āp na chheṛeṅ majlis meṅ , Inshā. In bātoṅ meṅ baiṭhe biṭhāë lākh bakheṛe paṛte hain . 8. Noise; bustle; uproar; tumult ( bahdā ). Bāzār meṅ kyā bakheṛā machā rakkhā hai . 9. Ways; arts; methods; resources. Ise kamāne ke bīsyoṅ bakheṛe āte haiṅ . 10. Goods; chattels; furniture; materials. 11. Rubbish; sweepings; scourings. 12. Task; affair; business ( dhandā ); worldly affairs.
part. Scattered, &c. (see bakhernā); — adj. Quarrelsome, &c. = bakheṛiyā.
adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, contentious, captious; — quarrelsome person, wrangler, brawler; mischief-maker, firebrand; rogue, knave, astute rascal.
more: (from بکهيڙا) adj. Quarrelsome, captious; a wrangler, a brawler.

def: n. m. 1. A mischief-maker; an incendiary; a firebrand ( āg-lagāū ). 2. A factious, quarrelsome person; a wrangler. 3. A consummate knave; rascal; rogue. 4. A cunning fellow; a hypocrite ( pākhanḍī ). 5. A juggler ( baṭṭe-bāz ); conjuror. bakheṛā chukānā , v. a. To settle a dispute ( jhagṛā miṭānā ); finish one's business. bakheṛā ḍāl'nā, karnā, yā machānā , v. a. See bakheṛā (1, 2, 4, 7, 8). To raise or make a difficulty; to set by the ears; to make a mess.
adj. & s.m. Miserly, avaricious, stingy; — miser, niggard (syn. kanjūs).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَخِیْل (baxiyl), from Arabic بَخِيل (baḵīl).

more: n. f. H. kanjūsī , q. v.

Bulāë na chalāë, mor tin bakhrā! E. Prov. Nor called nor even pronounced her name, yet in the feast three shares her claim. bakhrā kar'nā , E. bāṅṭ'nā , v. n. To distribute; allot; assign.

more: 1. part. act. (from بخشيدن) in compos. Giving, imparting, bestowing, forgiving; giver, forgiver. 2. s. m. Share, portion, lot, forgiveness. بخش دينا baḵẖsh denā, To give, to bestow, to forgive.

Paymaster and Commander-in-Chief. bakhshī-garī , n. f. The office of a general.

(from بخشيدن) s. f. Gift, donative, donation, grant, boon, reward, giving, beneficence, pardon, forgiveness, liberality, generosity. بخشش دينا baḵẖshish denā, To reward.

more: v. a. 1. To give; grant; bestow; confer; endow. 2. To excuse; forgive; pardon. 1. Bakhsho, bī billī, chūhā laṅḍūrā hī bhalā . Prov. Excuse me, cat, I am better as I am, a tailless rat. ☞ The rat having escaped to his hole with the loss of his tail, the cat tried to persuade him to come out again, vowing that she was only in play and she would put on his tail again very nicely if he would. 2. Tīn gunāh Khudā bhī bakhshtā hai . Prov. Saying. Even God forgives three times.
v. a. To give, to grant, to forgive, to pardon.

ety: Pehl. bakht; Zend bakhta, perf. part. rt. bag; S. bhakta, rt. bhaj

more: n. m. Ped. for A. nasīb ; P. bakhsh portion. Lot; portion; fortune; good fortune; luck; prosperity. Phr. A run of luck; the smiles of fortune. Bakhtoṅ ke balyā; pakāīkhīr, ho gayā dalyā! Prov. Hard fate, a rice-milk mess but now, To watery gruel turned somehow! bakht-āzmāī , n. f. Trial of fortune. bakht-āwar ; H. bhāgwān , adj. 1. Fortunate; lucky; propitious; prosperous. Bakht-āwar kā āṭā gīlā, kambakht kī dāl . Prov. The rich man's flour, the poor man's dāl (pulse) too thin. (The excess of water in the flour is corrected by adding more flour, while the deficiency of dāl must be made up by excess of water). 2. ( Ironic .) Unlucky; unfortunate.

s. m. Fortune, luck, prosperity, felicity. بخت آزمائي baḵẖt-āzmā-ī, s. f. Trial of fortune. بخت بازي baḵẖt-bāz-ī, s. f. Playing with fortune, trying oneʼs luck, chance. بخت بلي baḵẖt-balī, adj. Lucky, fortunate. بخت جلنا baḵẖt-jalnā, To lose oneʼs luck, to become unfortunate. بخت تيره baḵẖt-i-tīra or بخت سياه baḵẖt-i-siyāh, Misfortune.

Merit; worth; capacity; ability. 4. Power ( bal ); strength; might; reach. Kuchh bakhyā hai, to sāmne ā jā . (Slang). bakhyā'nā, bakhyā k . v. a. To stitch.
s. m. A kind of stitching, a strong quilting, stitching.

def: adj. & s.f. Remaining, lasting, enduring, permanent, existing, extant; eternal, everlasting; left in arrears, still due; — remaining portion, remainder, residue, remnant; overplus, surplus, excess; net produce; balance, dues, arrears, outstandings
more: n. f. بقي it remained. 1. The remaining portion; remnant; residue; what remains; what is left over. 2. The balance; dues; arrears; outstandings; balance of an account; sums receivable (baqāyā). Bāqī kā mārā gāoṅ, aur chilmoṅ kī mārī āg . Prov. The village is ruined with arrears, as the fire is put out by the pipe (with frequent demands upon it). 3. Overplus; surplus ( bachat ); excess; net produce. bāqī , adj. Imperishable; everlasting; eternal (H. amar ). Bāqī nām Allāh kā . Prov. When all hath perished, the name of the Everlasting shall remain. bāqī aīyām, bāqī ke din , n. m. The remaining period; unexpired term; the remainder of one's life. bāqī be-bāq karnā, yā chukānā , v. a. To pay up the balance due; liquidate a balance; square accounts; make good a default. bāqī paṛnā , v. n. To fall into arrears. bāqī chalī ātī hai , Balance lies over; arrears have accumulated. bāqī khazāna , n.…

part. act. (from بقا) Remaining, permanent, lasting, extant, eternal; (used substantively) remnant, remainder, balance, arrear. باقي دار bāḳī-dār, Owing a balance. باقي رهنا bāḳī rahnā, To remain, to be left, to be saved.
subs: s.m. Remaining period, unexpired term; remainder of (one's) life
s.m. Arrears of revenue, rent or taxes
adj. & s.m. Owing a balance; — one who owes a balance, one who is in arrears, defaulter; — bāqī-dārī, s.f. The owing a balance, the being in arrears
v.n. To remain, be over, be left, be saved; to remain unpaid or unadjusted, be still due; to have a balance
adj. & s.m. Remaining; residuary; — remainder, residue
(-kī), To find the difference (between two numbers), to subtract; to strike, or carry forward a balance; to shew a balance-sheet
ex: I will throw away the rest.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَاقِی (bāqī), from Arabic بَاقٍ (bāqin), from the root ب ق ي (b q y).

def: n. m. One who is in arrears, or owes a balance; a defaulter. bāqī rahnā, yā bachnā , v. n. To remain; to be left; to be due; to remain unpaid; accrue; have a balance. bāqī sāqī , n. f. Overplus; balance; remainder. bāqī farzī, bāqī barāë nām . A nominal balance. bāqī falāne kī . A balance in favour of such a one. bāqī kī muāfī . Remission of a balance. bāqī lagān , n. m. Arrears of rent. bāqī māl, yā mālguzārī . Arrears of revenue. bāqī māṅda . Remainder; residue. jāëdād-i-bāqī māṅda . Residuary estate. G. G. bāqī nikālnā , v. a. To strike or carry forward a balance; shew a balance sheet. bāqī wusūl karnā , v. a. To collect arrears.
= bāqilā, q.v. s.m. A bean, Faba sativa (of Jussieu), or Vicia faba (of Linnæus); pea; pot-herb.

part. act. f. (from بکر used substantively) A virgin.

s. f. Leavened bread of a particular shape baked in an oven ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

Balance of an account. zar-i-bāqī , n. m. A balance; remaining or outstanding balances. wusūl bāqī . Recovery of a balance; a balance.
s. plur. (of باقي) Remainders, balances, arrears.

adj. superl. (from بقل, used substantively) A grain-merchant, shopkeeper, grocer.

wety: From Arabic بَقَّار (baqqār).

s. m. (The stupid herbage) Purslain.

s.f. = bākhṛī, bākhlī, q.q.v. (i.e. bākh, q.v.+dim. aff. ṛī), s.f. lit. 'A young cow whose udder is developed'; a cow or buffalo gone about five months with calf; or which has calved about five months ago; (locally) a young cow or buffalo.
s.f. Boiled grain.
v.n. To prate, chatter, jabber, babble; to rave; to indulge in obscene or ribald talk; to rail (at).
more: v. n. S. वच् to speak. 1. To chatter; jabber; prate; babble. Merī chaṅdyā se pare haṭ-ke bakā-kar, māmā , Maiṅ ne yeh bāt kahī tujh ko hai sau bār, asīl . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Now, nurse, your clapper-tongue so near my head—take further off Your clatter, slave, I've told you now a hundred times hereof. 2. To indulge in obscene or smutty talk. 3. ( Slang ). To jaw; bark at; rail at; blow up; scold.
v. n. To prate, chatter, chat, jabber, babble, cackle.

more: (or pakolā ) s. m. A piece of blanket or of something else which needy faḳīrs put on their heads.

more: n. f. A green caterpillar, destructive of rice crops.

more: v. a. To lacerate with the nails, to claw, to scratch.

s.m. He-goat.

s. m. (dakh.) A sheep, a ram ( Harris ).

def: Eid al-Adha (Islamic feast of sacrifice)

s.f. = bākhṛī, q.v. (i.e. bākh, q.v.+dim. aff. ṛī), s.f. lit. 'A young cow whose udder is developed'; a cow or buffalo gone about five months with calf; or which has calved about five months ago; (locally) a young cow or buffalo.
adj. Crooked, curved, bent; entering on an apparently retrograde course (a planet)
subs: v.n. To retrograde, recede, go backward, enter on a retrograde course.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit बर्कर (barkara, “kid, lamb”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓- (-ika-).

def: s.f. She-goat; goat (generally); (met.), a great eater, gormandizer; idle or lazy fellow; simple person, simpleton; humble person.
more: n. f. S. वर्करी 1. A she-goat. 2. A gormandizer. Hāth, goṛ lakṛī, peṭ bakrī . Prov. His arms and legs thin as a stick, his stomach like a goat all day to pick. 3. An idle man; a lazy fellow. Khāne ko sher, kamāne ko bakrī . Prov. He feeds like a lion, but works like a goat. 4. A humble, poor, artless person; a simpleton. Bakrī hai, sāt pāṅch kuchh nahīṅ jāntā. bakrī honā , v. n. S. वक्र crooked. ( Ast .) To retrograde; recede; go backward; change the orbit or line of motion. Sanīchar bakrī ho gaya. bhīgī-bakrī ; P. tar-būz , n. f. (Enigmatically) A water melon. Rāt us sho/?/kh se maiṅ ne yeh pahelī meṅ kahā, Bhīgī bakrī kise kahte haiṅ, batāo to bhalā? Nazīr.
s. f. (dakh.) A sheep, an ewe ( Harris ).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit बर्कर (barkara, “kid, lamb”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓- (-ika-).

more: n. m. A box; a match box. baks-wālā , n. m. A pedlar; hawker.
subs: (or bakas-) wāla; — s.m. Boxman, pedlar, hawker, huckster (syn. pherī-wālā, bisātī).

s. m. The tongue of a buckle.

def: adj. & s.m. Speaking, talking; eloquent; talkative, loquacious, garrulous; — speaker (in general), talker; teacher, preacher, reader; talkative person, loquacious or garrulous person ( = bakwāsī).
more: n. m. S. वक्ता. 1. A speaker in general; preacher; reader ( qārī ). 2. A loquacious, garrulous person ( bakwāsī ).
n. m. S. मुष्टिक. 1. The act of clawing. Bandar ne bukṭā bhar līyā . A monkey clawed him. 2. A handful. Ek bukṭā ruī .
1. s. m. A speaker in general. 2. adj. Eloquent, loquacious, talkative.

Bektashi
Baku (the capital city of the Republic of Azerbaijan)

= H بکوادي बकवादी bakwādī [S. vāk+vādī], see s.v. bak.
more: (بک Prattle, and वाद Dispute) s. f. Prattle, foolish talk.

def: = H بکواد बकवाद bakwād [S. वाक्+वाद], see s.v. bak.
more: One who talks foolishly, an idle talker, prater, cackler.

more: m. (from بکنا) part. act. A chatterer, an idle talker.

more: v. a. Caus. of baknā , q. v.

def: Baal
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بَعْل (baʕl), from Proto-Semitic *baʕl-.

ety: S. बाला, rt. बल; cf. next
def: s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn
more: n. m. S. बाल. 1. Hair; wool. Buā, mere sir meṅ to itne bāl nahīṅ jo us se laṛūṅ . Mah. Wom. My dear, I have n't such a quantity of hair on my head that I can afford to fight with that virago. (She would pull all the hair off my head). 2. A crack (in glass or crockery). bāl utarnā , v. n. To be shaved. bāl ānā , v. n. 1. ( bāl paṛnā ). To crack (glass or crockery). Safāī uṛ gaī chehre kī jab khat kā nikāl āyā. Kahāṅ rahtī hai woh qimat jahāṅ chīnī meṅ bāl āyā . Gone is the smoothness of the face just as the hairs appear, The finest China's little worth if but a crack be there. 2. To have moustache or whiskers; to grow hairy. bāl bāl , adv. Every hair; hair by hair; entirely. Bāl bāl gunahgār hai . Prov. Saying. A sinner every hair on my body. bāl bāl bachnā , v. n. To win or escape by a hair; just to do it; escape with a whole skin. bāl bāl gaj-motī pironā , v. n. Poet. lit . To thread pe…
n. f. S. बल a sprout. The ear of corn.

n. m. S. बाला, Pālī. balo . An infant; a baby; child; boy or girl; a girl not yet arrived at the age of puberty. bāl-bachche ; Mar. ṭābar ṭūbar , n. m. Children; wife and children; family. "Āp-ke bāl bachche achchhe haiṅ?" "Sab kā bhurtā kareṅge." Latīfah. "Are all your family well?" "I'll make a mash of them." (Said by the deaf man who thought the other had asked him what he was going to make of the brinjals he was carrying in his hands). bāl-bachchoṅ-wālī , n. f. 1. A mother of a family; a mother. 2. The goddess of small-pox, so called because she carries off whom she will.

s. m. 1. Hair. 2. A boy, a child. 3. (for बाला ) s. f. A girl not exceeding sixteen. بال بال bāl bāl, Hair by hair, every hair. بال باندهي کوڙي مارنا bāl bāndhī kauṛī mārnā, To shoot at without missing, to act with great care, not to mistake. بال بيکا bāl bīkā, A bent or disordered hair, is used to express "the smallest harm or inconvenience;" so بال بيکانهووي bāl bīkā na howe, Let not a hair be disordered (let not the least harm be done). بال چرتر bāl-charitr, Tricks of children. بال گوپال bāl gopāl, Family.

s. m. A wing, a pinion. بال فشان bāl-fishān, Expanding the wings. بال افشاني bāl-afshānī or بال فشاني bāl-fishānī, s. f. Expansion or shaking of the wings. بال شکسته bāl-shikasta, Distressed, wretched.
s. f. 1. An ear or spike of corn. 2. A crack in a cup or glass. 3. s. m. The thread on which sugar is crystallized. بالدار bāl-dār, A cracked vessel (of china, glass, &c.).
s. m. Heart, mind: state: power.
subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: adj. Young, childish, infantine, juvenile, immature, not full grown; puerile, ignorant, uninstructed, unwise; — s.m. subs: s.f. State of a child, youth, infancy, childhood
v.n. To have hair coming on the face; — to have a crack (in, -meṅ), to be cracked (china, &c.)

adv. Hair by hair, every hair, altogether, throughout, entirely; — by a hair, by a hair's breadth, narrowly, barely; — bāl-bāl baćnā, v.n. To have a hair's breadth or narrow escape; barely to succeed or to win
To adorn oneself from head to foot, decorate oneself elaborately
v.n. To bind the hair; to braid the hair
adj. & adv., bāl-bāṅdhā-huʼā, adj. Minute, precise, exact; skilful, dexterous, expert; — to a hair, to a nicety; immediately, instantly; cheerfully
v.n. To shoot at without missing, to hit the mark; — to have true aim; to have great care, not to mistake
s.m. Children; wife and children, family; — bāl-baććoṅ-wālī, s.f. Mother of a family; — the goddess of small-pox (so named because she carries off whom she will)
s.f. Child-like intelligence, sense of a child
adj. & s.m. As much as a hair, the least or slightest; — hair's breadth, least bit
v.n. To let the hair grow
v.n. Letting the hair loose (a woman); to have the hair tossed or dishevelled; to be in a state of perplexity
v.n. To do the hair; to dress the hair; to shave
s.m. 'Instruction for the young'; a very small book (name of various works adapted to the capacity of the young)
s.m. An offering to Kr̤ishṇa presented in the early morning (syn. mohan-bhog)
Not a hair to be disordered; not to receive the slightest injury or harm
(-meṅ), To have a crack (in), be cracked (china, &c.)
s.m. Pimple, boil or sore (caused by plucking out or breaking a hair of the body)
v.n. Falling off of the hair
s.m. Childish exploits or tricks, juvenile sports or frolics; the sports of Kr̤ishṇa in his childhood
v.n. Pulling or picking out hairs
s.f. The hair on the crown of the head
s.m. A disease that causes the hair of horses to fall off ( = bād-ḵẖor)
adj. & s.m. Hairy, shaggy, hirsute; — cracked; a cracked cup or vessel

v.n. lit. 'To give the hair'; to be shaved preparatory to performing the obsequies of the dead (a Hindū rite, especially incumbent on the son); — to throw the hair of the head forward and backward before the taʻzīya of the Moharram festival (a ceremony performed by Mohammadan women)
s.f. Child-widow, a widow who loses her husband when she is very young
v.n. To allow the hair to grow
v.n. To let down the hair and disentangle it with a comb
s.m. 'Section treating of the boy Rāma,' the title of the first book of the Rāmāyaṇa
bāl-kī kammal banānā, lit. 'To make a sheep, or a blanket, of a single hair'; to magnify, exaggerate, make a mountain of a molehill
bāl-kī khāl khaiṅćnā To split hairs
s.m. Children, family ( = bāl-baćće); disciples, followers — bāl-ghāt, s.f. Infanticide ( = bāl-hatyā); — bāl-ghātak, s.m. Murderer of children
s.m. The act of tearing or plucking out the hair
v.n. To pull or pluck out the hair
(-kā), To tear or pull out the hair
s.f. Infanticide; — bāl-hatyārā, s.m. (f. -ī), Murderer of children
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: s.m. Wing, pinion: arm
subs: adj. Expanding the wings; — bāl-afshānī or fishānī, s.f. Expansion or shaking of the wings
v.n. lit. 'To be fledged'; to betray (one's) evil disposition, show what one is made of; to begin one's pranks; to turn over a new leaf; to flourish or prosper
adj. Having one's wings clipped; reduced to straits, distressed, wretched.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: s.m. State, condition, circumstances (e.g. fārig̠uʼl-bāl); heart, mind; power.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀮 (vāla), from Sanskrit वाल (vāla).

def: s.m. Power, strength, might, ability, vigour; force, violence, rigour, severity; effort, dint, virtue; support; military force, host, army; — semen virile; name of the elder brother of Krishna ( = bal-rām); — side, direction, way, wise ( = bhal); — difference (of quality, size, &c.); — adv. & postp. By dint (of, -ke), by virtue or power (of); supported (by), resting (on), on, (in comp.) -ward, -long (e.g. eṛī-ke bal ghūmnā, 'to turn round on the heel'; pīṭh-ke bal, 'on the back,' 'backward'; ćhaṛi-ke-bal jhuknā, 'to lean on a stick'; sir-ke bal, māthe-ke bal, 'headlong, head-foremost'; khambh-ke bal khaṛā honā, 'to be supported on a column, to stand on a column'; muṅh-ke bal, 'on the face, forward')
more: n. m. S. वल् to go round. See اکرَ (1, 2, 4, & 6 to 9). 1. Crookedness; obliquity; tortuosity; distortion. Mujh se aiṅṭhe to ghaṛī bhar meṅ bal nikāl dūṅgā . If you turn rusty, I'll soon take the twist out of you. 2. Twist; twirl; coil; convolution; bend; curvature; sinuosity; windings and turnings. 1. Kāṅdhe pe dāl, ātā, haldī girah ke bal meṅ, Hāthoṅ meṅ ghī kī piyālī, aur lakṛīyāṅ bagal meṅ. Barsāt . Rains. Nazīr. His dāl and flour upon his shoulders slung, Some turmeric tied up in a knot is swung, Both hands enfold a cup of ghee, held tight His fire-wood under both his arms, poor wight. (One hand is placed on the open cup or leaf platter to protect it from kites). 2. Rassī jal gaī, par bal na gayā . Prov. The singed rope its twist retains. (Reduced to poverty, he still affects his old pomp). 3. The fold of the skin in stout persons upon the upper part of the belly, between the ensifo…

s. m. 1. A coil, twist, crook, convolution. 2. Side, direction. See H. بهل bhal. بلدار bal-dār, adj. Crooked, twisted, coiled, convoluted. بل دينا bal denā, v. a. To twist. بل کهانا bal khānā, To be twisted.

s. m. 1. ( बल ) A name of the elder brother of Kr̤ish̤ṇā. 2. Power, strength. بل بي bal be, interj. Bravo! 3. or bali ( बलि ) Name of the king of Pātāla or the infernal regions. 4. A sacrifice, an offering. بل جانا bal jānā or بل بل جانا bal bal jānā, To be sacrificed. بل دينا bal denā, or بل کرنا bal karnā, To sacrifice.
subs: (S. bala+abala), s.m. Strength and weakness, comparative strength and want of strength; relative importance and insignificance
adv. With great effort, or energy, or ardour, ardently, zealously
adj. & s.m. Strong, powerful; — a strong or robust man; name of Krishna's elder brother Balrām
s.m. Taking violent or forcible possession of another's right
adj. & s.m. Brave, heroic, valourous; the brave, &c.
s.m. The male palm, the toddy tree, Borassus flabelliformis
adj. Possessing strength, strong, powerful ( = balwān)
s.m. Air, wind; name of the elder brother of Krishna ( = bal-rām); — a cowherd
s.f. A species of medicinal plant (commonly called trāyamān)
s.m. Name of the elder brother of Krishna
s.m. A person put in charge of property under distraint for arrears of revenue
v.n. To put forth strength, to make an effort
adj. & s.m. Strong, powerful ( = balwān); a strong man
s.f. Science of forces, mechanics (syn. ʻilm-ě-jar-ě-saqīl)
s.m. lit. 'Destroyer of strength'; phlegm; the phlegmatic humour
adj. Destitute of strength, weak, infirm
s.f. The being destitute of strength, weakness, exhaustion, prostration of strength, infirmity (whether from fatigue or age).
wety: From Sanskrit बल (bala), from Proto-Indo-European *bel- (“power, strength”).

def: conj. But, yet, &c. = balki, q.v. conj. Moreover, but, nay, nay but, nay rather, on the contrary.
wety: From Sanskrit बल (bala), from Proto-Indo-European *bel- (“power, strength”).

def: immediate family, parents and their children
s. f. A widow who when very young lost her husband.
s.f. Female child, girl, young woman (not more than sixteen years of age); woman; — small cardamoms; a medicinal and fragrant plant, Hibiscus tortuosus (cf. bāl-ćhaṛ).
more: n. m. S. बाला A child. See بال 1. "Ā jā rī niṅdyā, tū ā kyoṅ na jā, Mere bāle kī āṅkhoṅ meṅ ghul mil jā." "Ātī hūṅ, bīwī, ātī hūṅ, Do chār bāle sulātī hūṅ." Lorī . Lullaby. "Come, hie away, sleep, and tarry nowise, Melt and dissolve in dear baby's eyes." "Yes, lady, I come, I come with a sweep, Some three or four babies I'm putting to sleep." 2. Buḍḍhe kī na mare jorū, bāle kī na mare māṅ . Prov. May the old man's wife ne'er die, and the young babe's mother live for aye. 3. Tātī tātī pūryāṅ, ghīyā chapoṛiyāṅ, Maiṅ khāūṅ, merā bālā khāë, dhar kān maṛoṛi- yāṅ . Children's game. Hot hot cakes with butter drip For me and my child—on your ear a clip.

adj. 1. Infantile; childish; young; boyish; girlish. Sir gālā, mūṅh bālā . Prov. The hairs of his head as white as cotton, yet the words of his mouth are very childishness. 2. Budding; youthful; blooming. Najrā gaīṅ bālam tore aṅgnā, Tore lākh rupaīye, merā bālā jubnā . Song. Thumrī . An evil eye, my love, fell on me, in your yard, no other, What's one lac in one scale against my youth's bloom in the other. 3. Ignorant; unknowing; uninstructed. bālā-bholā , adj. Childlike; innocent; artless; simple. bāle bhole , n. m. Children; babies. Gul, shor "bāle bhole, khilaune kī hai bahār!" kyā kyā maze hain īd ke, āj īd-gāh meṅ! Nazir. Some shout "for children here of toys a brilliant store," 'Tis Id—what founts of pleasure this day well and pour! bālā chāṅd, nayā chāṅd ; A. hilāl , P. māh-i-nau , n. m. The new, or young moon. bālī umar , n. f. Youth; the prime of life.
n. m. 1. A grub that eats young plants of wheat or barley when about six inches high. 2. A medicinal and fragrant plant of the roots of which lattices are made; Andropogon muricatum . Wat.

n. m. bālī ; Tir. bīṛī; E. bārī ; Maṛ. muṛkī , n. f. S. वलय, H. bal a twist. An ear-ring. Kyā khūb chamaktā hai tere kān kā bālā, Gardan surāhī-dār, badan sāṅche meṅ ḍhālā . How brightly glitter on your ear your earrings gold, A neck like goblet arched, a form cast in a mould.

prep. 1. On; upon; up. 2. Foregoing; above or before-mentioned. bālā-bālā , adv. Ped. for H. ūpar hī ūpar . Apart; away from; slily; secretly. bālā-bar , n. m. Part of the aṅgarkhā , hence a kind of aṅgarkhā. bālā-posh, lihāf' , n. m. A coverlet; a quilt. bālā-khānā; H. aṭārī , n. m. An upper room; a balcony. bālā-dast , adj. Superior; higher. bālā-gashtī , n. f. Patrolling; going one's beat or rounds; inspection ( nigrānī ). bālā-nashīṅ , n. m. lit . Seated aloft. 1. The chief seat; the upper end of the table. Kam kharch, bālā-nashīṅ . Prov. Small cost, great show. 2. One who occupies the chief seat; a president; chairman. mutazak'kira bālā , Ped. The above-mentioned or aforesaid.

prep. Above, up, aloft. adj. High. s. m. Top, stature. بالابالا bālā bālā, adv. Deceitfully, fraudulently: secretly. بالابتانا bālā batānā, or بالادينا bālā denā, To balk, to circumvent, to dupe, to put off, to disappoint, to prevaricate. بالادست bālā-dast, adj. Upper-handed, victorious, superiour, more or most precious.
1. s. f. A female child, a girl not exceeding sixteen. 2. A medicinal and fragrant plant. 3. s. m. An earring. 4. (for Sans. बाल ) A male child, a boy. بالاچاند bālā chānd, The new moon.
s. Andropogon muricatum ( Qanoon-e-islam ), or Andropogon schœnanthus ( Wilson ).

def: adj. & s.m. Young, childlike, childish, &c.; child, infant, boy, &c. ( = bāl, s.m.); — a grub that eats young plants of wheat or barley (when about six inches high); — a medicinal and fragrant plant, Hibiscus mutabilis; — a large earring
subs: adj. Childlike, innocent, artless, simple, guileless; — bāle-bhole, s.m. pl. Children, little folk, babes
s.m. The rays of the rising sun
s.f. Youth, prime of life, &c. = bāl-avasthā).

def: s.m. Top, upper part; stature; evasion; delusion; — adj. & adv. High, lofty, elevated, exalted; up, aloft, above, foregoing, before-mentioned; — prep. On, upon, above ( = ūpar)
subs: adv. Apart, away from; secretly, stealthily ( = ūpar-hī ūpar, q.v.)
(-ko), bālā-buttā denā (-ko), To evade, elude by stratagem; to delude, deceive, cheat
s.m. Part of the angarkhā that laps over the thighs, fore-skirt; a kind of angarkhā
s.m. A part of dress; a kind of turban; a coat of a particular kind
s.m. Upper room; upper story; balcony
adj. Higher, superior; having the upper hand; — bālā-dast honā (-par), To prevail (against), be victorious (over), be superior (to); to have power or authority (over)
s.f. Going one's beat or rounds, patrolling; inspection
adj. & s.m. Seated aloft, occupying the chief seat; — occupier of the chief seat, president, chairman; — the chief seat; the upper end (of a table, &c.)
s.m. & adv. The heavens above and the earth beneath; — above and below.

def: s.f. The medicinal plant Sida cordifolia, or S. rhomboides, or S. rhombifolia.
more: n. f. 1. Calamity. See اَفت (1). 1. Rāt kyā ātī hai, ek sar pe balā ātī hai . 2. Idhar na udhar, ye balā kidhar? Prov. This evil here nor there, where is it then? (This wretch will neither die nor get well). 3. Tawele kī balā bandar ke sir . Prov. Whatever goes wrong in the stable the monkey pays for all. ☞ Monkeys are kept in stables as a protection against any calamity. 2. A wrangling jade; a caution; a hag; witch ( ḍāin ). Balā sī woh dekh uske pīchhe paṛī . Mīr Hasan. 3. A firebrand; fury; demon; fiend. 4. An evil spirit. Kis balā ne mere bachche ko toṛ khāyā? Wom. 5. An insignificant or contemptible thing ( hech ); one's shoe, thumb, toe, etc. 1. Tū kyā balā hai! 2. Lohā jāne, luhār jāne, dhoṅkne-wāle kī balā jāne . Prov. Let the iron answer, let the blacksmith answer, what the d—has the bellowsman to do with it? 6. ( balā bo/?/gmā ). Trash; rubbish. Ye kyā balā bogmā uṭhā lāī! Mah.…

adj. 1. Horrible; frightful; dreadful. 2. Savage; furious; infuriate; foaming. 3. Wrangling; quarrelsome; pugnacious. Rāt din balā sī pīchhe paṛī rahtī hai . 4. Sharp; quick; smart. Iskā zahn balā hai . 5. Excessive; inordinate; too much. Āj to balā mircheṅ ḍālīṅ haiṅ .
Hin. Wom. n. m. S. बलय An ornament; armlet.
s. m. A medicinal plant. (Sida rhombifolia, rhomboidea and cordifolia).

s. f. Trial, calamity, misfortune, accident, vengeance, evil, ill. بلازده balā-zada, adj. Overwhelmed with misfortune. بلاکش balā-kash, Afflicted, sufferer. بلاگردان balā-gardān, s. m. What averts evil; expiation, sacrifice. بلانصيب balā-naṣīb, adj. Unfortunate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: s.f. Trial, affliction, misfortune, calamity, evil, ill; a person or thing accounted a trial, affliction, &c.; evil genius, evil spirit, devil, fiend; a wonderful or extraordinary person or thing; an awful or terrible person or thing; an insignificant, or vile, person or thing; excessive, fearful or awful amount or quantity (of)
subs: balā-nosh, s.m. One who eats anything vile or unclean; one who eats offerings and sacrifices; one who eats anything indiscriminately
lit. 'Far be the evil'; a phrase used to avert an evil omen
adj. Stricken with misfortune or evil, overwhelmed with calamity
adj. Trying, troublesome; fearful, awful, terrible; an excessive or awful amount or quantity of
adj. & s.m. Tried, afflicted; — afflicted person, sufferer
adj. & s.m. Warding off or averting evil; — what averts evil; expiation, sacrifice

balāʼeṅ lenā (-kī), To draw the hands over the head of another (and then crack the fingers over one's own temples) in token of taking all his (or her) misfortunes upon oneself (a practice among women = balaiyāṅ lenā); to devote oneself for another (syn. ṣadqe honā)

s.m. = balā-ćaṭ, q.v. (N.B. The word balā is often used most idiomatically in a manner difficult to be rendered in English; e.g. tumhārī balā-se, sc. kām hai, lit. 'It concerns your evil genius'; it is no concern of yours; what is it to do with you, never mind
'What awful thing are you?' Who cares for you? You are of no significance
'What fearful or trying work'; what a fearful amount of work
āj to balā mirćeṅ ḍālīṅ hain
'They have put in a fearful or tremendous quantity of chillies to-day.')
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: s.m. Bracelet; armlet ( = bal.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: Above and below, i. e. the heavens above and the earth beneath.
adv. In or on the lip, at or to the lip.
wety: Borrowed from English bulb.

def: prop. n. The city of Balbeck in Syria, the ancient Heliopolis.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَعْلَبَکّ (ba'labakk), from Arabic بَعْلَبَكّ (baʕlabakk).

def: s.m. See s.v. bal, 'power.' s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn
subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.

def: s. m. A part of dress, a kind of turband: a coat of a particular kind.
s.m. A kind of jacket (syn. ćapkan).
more: s. (dakh. Qanoon-e-islam ) A kind of vest: see چپکن chapkan.

def: s. m. Part of the انگرکها angarkhā that laps over the thighs, foreskirt.
adj. Innocent, artless as a child. s. m. An artless child.
s.m. City, town; country.
s.m. Bull; bullock, &c. ( = barad, baradh, qq.v.); horned cattle; burden-bearing bullock.
s.m. Tax on laden oxen.
s. m. A name of Balarāma.
v.t. To bull (a cow).
s.m. The marking-nut, Semecarpus anacardium (syn. bhilāwan).
To draw the hands over the head of another in token of taking all his misfortunes upon oneʼs self. (Generally practised by women).

ety: for A. بلاغة, inf. n. of بلغ 'to be eloquent,' &c.
def: s.f. Eloquence; rhetoric; — maturity.
adj. Simple, foolish, silly, of little sense, without discrimination; weak in intellect; ignorant; unskilful ( = ablah, q.v.).
more: adj. Weak, silly, simple, ignorant, unskilful.

def: s.m. A low caste servant, a village guide, messenger, or watchman; a caste of fishermen and basket-makers.
W. n. m. 1. A caste of fishermen and basket-makers. 2. A village guide, messenger, or watchman.
s.m. A low caste of ćamārs or workers in hides and leather; a member of that caste (he is also occasionally employed to measure land).
n. A low caste of chamārs or workers in hides and leather; a cobbler, sometimes employed to measure land.
= ulahnā, q.v. s.m. Twitting, casting in one's teeth; reproach; complaint, accusation ( = ulahnā)
subs: v.t. To twit, taunt, reproach; to accuse, complain against.

def: adv. & s.m. Additional, extra, unusual, extraordinary; extraneous; general; — in addition to, over and above; — anything extra or additional; extra tax; a horse that is led for show; — s.f. That which is on the surface; cream ( = malāʼī)
more: n. m. Evasion; putting off; delusion. bālā batānā, bālā buttā denā , v. a. 1. To evade; put off; elude by stratagem. Khawāsoṅ ko bālā batānā use, Akele darakhtoṅ meṅ jānā use . Mīr Hasan. Escaping from her handmaids on some plea, Alone in groves poured forth her misery. 2. To dupe; cheat; circumvent; deceive.

adv. In addition to; over and above. bālā'ī , n. f. What is on the surface; cream ( malāī ). bālāī paidā, āmad, yā yāft, ūprī paidā , n. f. 1. Fees; perquisites; emoluments; pickings. Is naukrī meṅ kuchh bālāī yāft bhī hai? 2. Bribes ( rishvat ); illegal gratifications. Kachehrī kī naukrī meṅ bālāī paidā bahut hai . There are nice pickings to be had in the Courts.
(A led horse); adj. Over and above, extraordinary. بالائي مزي bālā-ī maze, s. plur. Secret pleasures. بالائي يافت bālā-ī yāft, s. f. Perquisites.
subs: bālāʼī paidā, bālāʼī yāft, s.f. Emoluments, fees, perquisites, pickings; bribes
s.m. General, or miscellaneous, duties; odd jobs ( = ūpar-kā kām)
s.m. Superficial, or secret, pleasures; outward enjoyment and pleasure of every kind short of carnal intercourse (with a woman).

def: s.f. Benediction, blessing ( = asīr-bād); boon ( = bar).
s.m. Name of a disease; — name of a forest in Faiẓābād.
To draw oneʼs hands over the head of another in token of taking his misfortunes on oneʼs self. See بلاٴين لينا.

r: balaiyā, balā, balaiyā lenā, balāʼeṅ lenā
def: (A. بليه) = balā, q.v.
subs: q.v.s.v. balā. s.f. Female child, girl, young woman (not more than sixteen years of age); woman; — small cardamoms; a medicinal and fragrant plant, Hibiscus tortuosus (cf. bāl-ćhaṛ).

def: s.m. Child, boy, &c. = bāl, q.v.
more: n. m. A child. See بال 1. Kharbūzāh chāhe dhūp ko, aur ām chāhe meṅh, Nārī chāhe zor ko, aur bālak chāhe neṅh . Prov. The melon wants the sun, the mango thirsts for rain; For woman a strong mate, but children love will chain. 2. Baṛā jāne kīyā, bālak jāne hīyā . Prov. The adult looks to deeds, the child love only heeds. bālakoṅ kī sudh na rakhnā . Neglect of children; desertion of one's family. G. G.
s. m. A young child, an infant.
subs: To be neglectful of (one's) children or family; to desert (one's) family.

def: s.f. A crane.
s.f. = bālakpan, q.v. s.m. Childhood, &c. = bāl-pan and bālavasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
s. m. An upper room, an upper story, a balcony.
adj. Relating to children; infantine; childish.
v.n. To speak indistinctly (through excessive joy, or through intoxication); to mouth (one's) words; to bubble, boil ( = ubalnā); — v.t. To open.
more: v. a. 1. To open. 2. To speak indistinctly from excessive joy or intoxication.

def: s.m. Childhood, &c. = bāl-pan and bālavasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
s.m. Childhood, &c. = bāl-pan and bālavasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
s.m. Flacourtia cataphracta; Carissa carondas.
s.m. Lover, beloved person, sweetheart; husband; — a kind of cloth
more: Poet. n. m. S. वल्लभ a favourite; H. bal strength, mā , S. मम, , P. man mine. 1. A lover; paramour; beloved; sweet-heart. 2. A husband; spouse; consort. bālam chāur , n. m. A kind of rice. Ghughuā-ghū maneryā, bālam chaurā ḍheryā, Bābū khāë dudh-bhattā, bilaīyā chāṭe pattā . Mag. Nursery Song. Have a ride, darling, on my knee, rice-heaps of trodden sheaf, My baby feeds on milk and rice, the cat licks up the leaf. ☞ The natives usually eat off a leaf or leaves stitched together when a plate is not procurable.
s. m. 1. A lover or beloved person; a husband. 2. A kind of cloth. بالم کهيرا bālam khīrā, s. m. A sort of cucumber in season in the rains (a variety of Cucumis sativus).
subs: s.m. A superior kind of rice
s.m. A superior kind of white cucumber (in season in the rains; a variety of Cucumis sativus).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वल्लभतम (vallabhatama). Cognate with Gujarati વાલમ (vālam).

def: s.m. Lover; husband.
more: s. m. A lover, a husband.

def: n. m. A superior white cucumber. Kaddū * kāṭ mirdaṅg banāyā, nībū * kāṭ majīrā, Sāt turaīyāṅ * maṅgal gāweṅ, nāche bālam-khīrā . * The pumpkin cut a drum did make, and two half limes the cymbals; The seven turais for joy did sing, the lithe gourd lively gambols.
s.m. Volunteer.
s.f. The lute of Rāma.
s.m. A species of citron = bādrang, q.v. s.m. Orange; kind of citron; a species of cucumber
more: n. m. A kind of citron.
subs: or A. bād-ranj-būya, s.m. Mountain-balm, Arabian balm; citronel.

def: part. Occupying the chief seat; a president.
adj. Possessed of power, powerful, strong; leading an army.
adj. Cheaply purchased, bought at a rate far below the real value, dirt-cheap; — s.m. A great bargain.
more: n. m. S. विडाल, Palī. bilālo, bilāro . A tom-cat ( billā ).

def: s.m. = bāl-pan, bālak-pan, and bāl-avasthā, q.q.v. s.m. Infancy, childhood, boyhood, minority = bālak-pan and bāl-avasthā.
s. m. A coverlet, a quilt.
s.m. A particular disease, a kind of consumptive expectoration; the phlegmatic humour.
s.m. Balsam, balm of Gilead.
s. f. High-headedness.
s.m. A kind of bean, Phaseolus mungo.
